Full Guide

14-Day Gansu & Qinghai Danxia, Desert and Plateau Lakes Nature Route

Lanzhou - Zhangye - Jiayuguan - Dunhuang - Dachaidan - Chaka - Qinghai Lake - Xining

This handbook is written directly for international visitors planning a nature-focused journey across northwest China. It is not a general sightseeing article. It is an actionable 14-day route guide covering foreigner-friendly accommodation choices, intercity and scenic-area transport, daily sightseeing order, dining recommendations, estimated budgets, backup plans and practical reminders. All prices are reference prices only. Final costs should be checked again according to exact travel dates on official platforms, Trip.com, Ctrip, 12306, hotel front desks, scenic-area ticket counters or licensed drivers.

Start planningSee highlights
Booking-Ready Handbook

Complete route planning in one page

This web version preserves the full content of the paid guide while presenting it in a modern, responsive layout for easier reading on desktop and mobile.

Basic Information

This handbook is written directly for international visitors planning a nature-focused journey across northwest China. It is not a general sightseeing article. It is an actionable 14-day route guide covering foreigner-friendly accommodation choices, intercity and scenic-area transport, daily sightseeing order, dining recommendations, estimated budgets, backup plans and practical reminders. All prices are reference prices only. Final costs should be checked again according to exact travel dates on official platforms, Trip.com, Ctrip, 12306, hotel front desks, scenic-area ticket counters or licensed drivers.

Basic InformationDetails
Route ThemeNorthwest China nature landscapes: ink-style Danxia, rainbow Danxia, Gobi desert, sand dunes, wind-eroded Yardang landforms, salt lakes, plateau lakes and red-rock lake valleys.
Recommended Duration14 days / 13 nights
Entry / ExitEnter from Lanzhou and leave from Xining. The route can also be done in reverse if flight schedules are better.
Suitable ForInternational visitors who want to focus on China’s northwest scenery, photographers, geology lovers, couples, small groups and families who can accept long driving distances.
Best SeasonsMay-June and September-October are generally the most comfortable. July-August has stronger tourist demand and higher prices. Winter is quieter but colder, with more weather risk.
DifficultyModerate to moderately demanding. No extreme hiking is required, but the route includes long road transfers, dry climate, strong sun, wind, desert walking and some high-altitude lake areas.
Transport LogicHigh-speed rail for major city-to-city segments; taxis or hired cars for scenic areas; hired car is recommended from Dunhuang into the Qinghai lake section.
Exchange Rate UsedUSD 1 approximately RMB 7.2. USD amounts are rounded estimates.

Designed for first-time international visitors

Route Highlights

Lanzhou Ink Danxia Scenic Area
Zhangye Bell and Drum Tower
Mogao Caves at Sunset in Dunhuang
Dunhuang Cave Statues and Cultural Relic Exhibition
Qinghai Lake
Summer Train on Chaka Salt Lake

1. Route Logic and Nature Landscape Theme

This route is designed around three major northwest-China landscapes: red Danxia and colorful sedimentary hills, desert and wind-eroded landforms, and salt lakes plus highland lakes. The first half in Gansu focuses on Lanzhou Ink Danxia, Zhangye Colorful Danxia, Mingsha Mountain and Crescent Spring, the Mogao Caves environment and Dunhuang Yardang. The second half enters Qinghai and focuses on Dachaidan Emerald Lake, Chaka Salt Lake, Qinghai Lake and optional Kanbula.

The biggest difference between this route and southern China nature routes is scale. Distances are long, towns are far apart, and the road scenery often includes Gobi desert, Qilian Mountain foothills, basins, salt flats and plateau lake areas. You should treat this as a long-distance nature road journey rather than a compact city trip. Book foreigner-friendly hotels in advance, use licensed drivers or reputable booking platforms for remote sections, and avoid night driving on long desert roads.

PlaceUnique Natural AppealTravel Advice
Lanzhou Ink DanxiaSoft red sandstone hills, valleys and layered ridges. It is less brightly colored than Zhangye and works well as a gentle Danxia introduction.Half day is enough. Afternoon light is better for layers and long views. If wind or visibility is poor, switch to the Yellow River waterfront.
Zhangye Colorful DanxiaRed, yellow, white, grey and green mineral bands spread across rolling hills like an exposed geological cross-section.Schedule the core visit 1.5-2 hours before sunset and use the scenic shuttle to move between viewing platforms.
Binggou Danxia / Pingshan Lake Grand CanyonBinggou is more about castle-like rock shapes; Pingshan Lake is more about walking inside a canyon with stairs and close rock walls.Ordinary visitors should choose Binggou. Strong walkers who want a canyon experience can choose Pingshan Lake.
Mingsha Mountain and Crescent SpringHigh sand dunes, desert light, camel routes and a crescent-shaped oasis spring appear in the same scene.Enter in late afternoon to avoid heat. Camel riding, sand-hill climbing and sunset viewing are the main activities.
Mogao CavesA protected World Heritage cave-art site set in the Dunhuang oasis environment. The visit requires strict reservation and conservation rules.Book early. Follow the official visiting sequence and never touch walls, paintings or sculptures.
Dunhuang YardangWind-eroded desert formations shaped like fleets, castles and animals across an open Gobi landscape.Best done with a driver or regulated day tour. It is far from town; do not self-arrange casually without transport confirmation.
Dachaidan Emerald LakeSalt ponds with blue, green and dark mineral colors. On clear, windless days, the lake looks like a patchwork of gemstones.Best in good sunlight and low wind. Bring sunglasses and avoid stepping onto unsafe salt crust.
Chaka Salt LakeA salt lake famous for mirror-like reflections when the sky is clear, wind is low and shallow water conditions are right.Do not expect mirror reflections on windy or cloudy days. White salt surfaces can be very bright; protect eyes and skin.
Qinghai LakeChina’s largest lake, with a sea-like water surface, plateau grassland, distant mountains and seasonal rapeseed flowers.Do not rush it as a photo stop only. The value is the scale of lake, sky, grassland and road scenery.

2. 14-Day Route Overview

DayRouteMain Nature ExperienceOvernight
Day 1Arrive in LanzhouArrival, rest, payment/network check, easy Yellow River walkLanzhou
Day 2Lanzhou Ink Danxia + Yellow River waterfrontDanxia warm-up, viewing platforms, Yellow River sunsetLanzhou
Day 3Lanzhou - ZhangyeHigh-speed rail transfer, supplies and easy city restZhangye
Day 4Zhangye Colorful Danxia + Binggou / Pingshan LakeColorful hills, sunset platforms, shape Danxia or canyon walkingZhangye
Day 5Zhangye - JiayuguanHexi Corridor transfer, Gobi-edge rest and suppliesJiayuguan
Day 6Jiayuguan - Dunhuang + Mingsha MountainDesert dunes, Crescent Spring and sunsetDunhuang
Day 7Mogao Caves + Dunhuang recoveryWorld Heritage cave-art site and oasis restDunhuang
Day 8Dunhuang Yardang / Yumen Pass directionWind-eroded Yardang landforms and Gobi sceneryDunhuang
Day 9Dunhuang - DachaidanLong transfer from Gobi desert to Qaidam BasinDachaidan
Day 10Dachaidan Emerald LakeBlue-green salt ponds and mineral-color lake sceneryDachaidan / Delingha
Day 11Dachaidan / Delingha - Chaka Salt LakeSalt-lake mirror landscape and plateau drivingChaka / Qinghai Lake area
Day 12Chaka - Qinghai LakeLarge lake, grassland, mountains and seasonal flowersQinghai Lake / Xining
Day 13Qinghai Lake - Xining; optional KanbulaFinal buffer day; optional red-rock lake valleyXining
Day 14Depart from XiningDeparture day; no distant scenic area-

3. Core Price Reference Table (RMB + USD)

AreaAttraction / ItemRMBUSDNotes
LanzhouInk Danxia ticket + shuttleRMB 80-120USD 11-17Half-day Danxia warm-up; package prices vary by platform and season.
LanzhouYellow River cable car / cruise / small viewing projectsRMB 30-120USD 4-17Optional. If tired, just walk along the river.
ZhangyeColorful Danxia ticket + shuttleRMB 93-120USD 13-17Adult ticket + shuttle reference; sunset periods are most popular.
ZhangyeBinggou DanxiaRMB 60-100USD 8-14Shape-focused Danxia; easier than a long canyon walk.
ZhangyePingshan Lake Grand CanyonRMB 130-200USD 18-28More stairs and walking; better for fit visitors.
DunhuangMingsha Mountain and Crescent SpringRMB 110-120USD 15-17Camel riding, shoe covers and small projects are extra.
DunhuangCamel ride at Mingsha MountainRMB 100-150USD 14-21Optional. Avoid peak heat and protect phone/camera from sand.
DunhuangMogao Caves normal / emergency ticketRMB 100-300USD 14-42Reservation rules vary by season and quota. Book early.
DunhuangYardang Geopark / Yumen Pass directionRMB 50-200USD 7-28Often combined with long-distance transport.
DachaidanEmerald LakeRMB 50-120USD 7-17Prices vary by shuttle and platform.
ChakaChaka Salt Lake ticket / shuttle / train optionsRMB 60-200USD 8-28Reflection depends on weather and water conditions.
Qinghai LakeErlangjian or paid viewing areaRMB 90-150USD 13-21Some roadside lake viewpoints are operated separately.
KanbulaKanbula scenic area packageRMB 100-250USD 14-35Optional Day 13 add-on; requires more driving time.
Price note: Northwest scenic tickets, shuttles, camel rides, tourist trains, hired cars and package tickets vary significantly by season, holiday policy and booking platform. Use the table for route budgeting and check again before departure.

4. Scenic Highlights, Must-See Points and Internal Activities

AttractionMost Distinctive FeatureMust-See PointWhat You Can Do InsideSuggested TimeBackup Plan
Lanzhou Ink DanxiaSoft red sandstone hills, ravines and distant layers.Jiuse Danqing, Silk Road sunset-style viewpoints and wide ridgeline views.Take scenic shuttle between platforms; photograph layers, valleys and distant hills. Deep hiking is not necessary.3-5 hrsIf windy or hazy, switch to Yellow River waterfront, Baita Mountain viewpoint or hotel rest.
Zhangye Colorful DanxiaMulticolored sedimentary layers like a geological layer cake.Sunset platforms, color-banded valleys and Qilian Mountain background.Use the shuttle route; focus on panoramic platforms; do not try to walk between distant platforms.3-4 hrsIf cloudy, still visit for structure; if sandstorm, shorten the stay.
Binggou DanxiaCastle, palace and column-shaped red rock formations.Louvre-like formations, camel-shaped rocks and palace outlines.Shuttle + short walks; best paired with Colorful Danxia to compare color versus shape.2.5-4 hrsFit visitors may choose Pingshan Lake instead; do not combine both if tired.
Pingshan Lake Grand CanyonDeep canyon, red walls and close-up rock corridors.Canyon floor perspective, red cliffs and narrow passages.Walk down/up stairs, enter the valley floor, photograph rock walls close-up; protect knees.4-5 hrsOlder visitors, families or low-energy travelers should choose Binggou.
Mingsha Mountain and Crescent SpringSand dunes and a crescent-shaped oasis spring in one desert scene.Sunset from the dune ridge and overlooking Crescent Spring from above.Camel ride, sand-hill climbing, shoe-cover rental, sunset photography.3-5 hrsIf wind is strong, keep to lower areas and skip climbing high dunes.
Mogao CavesProtected Silk Road cave art with statues and murals.Officially opened caves, digital exhibition and guide-led cave visit.Watch the digital introduction, follow the guided cave route, observe murals and sculptures without touching.3-4 hrsIf normal tickets sell out, try emergency tickets or switch to Dunhuang Museum/rest.
Dunhuang YardangWind-eroded desert formations shaped by long-term erosion.Fleet-like, castle-like and animal-like Yardang silhouettes at sunset.Take scenic shuttle/driver route, photograph silhouettes and Gobi horizons.6-8 hrs incl. transportIf weather is poor, shorten the route and return before dark.
Dachaidan Emerald LakeBlue, green and dark mineral salt ponds in a basin landscape.Windless reflections, color blocks and Qaidam desert edges.Walk designated paths, photograph reflections, compare different pond colors.2-4 hrsIf windy/cloudy, focus on color contrast rather than reflection.
Chaka Salt LakeMirror-like salt lake reflections on clear windless days.White salt surface, sky reflection and small tourist train if operating.Walk on safe designated areas, take reflection photos, use shoe covers if required.2-4 hrsIf weather is poor, shorten stay and continue to Qinghai Lake area.
Qinghai LakeVast lake surface, plateau grassland, road scenery and distant mountains.Erlangjian/official viewing area, lakeside road views, seasonal rapeseed fields.Lake photography, short lakeside walk, grassland view stops; avoid unsafe private lake access.3-5 hrsIf weather is bad, make it a scenic drive and sleep in Xining.
KanbulaRed-rock cliffs, reservoir/lake valley and forested Danxia landscape.Red cliffs above water, valley viewpoints and road scenery.Drive between viewpoints, short walks and lake-valley photography.4-6 hrs + transportSkip if Day 14 departure is early or if weather/traffic is poor.

5. Accommodation Guide: Foreigner-Friendly Hotels and Standard-Room Price Ranges

This table only recommends hotels that are more suitable for international visitors: international chains, higher-star city hotels, or hotels commonly bookable on major international platforms. Still confirm passport check-in before payment, especially in Dachaidan, Chaka and Qinghai Lake areas.

DestinationRecommended Hotel / Room TypeLocationWhy RecommendedForeigner Check-In ReliabilityLow SeasonRegular SeasonPeak Season
LanzhouHyatt Regency Lanzhou / Hilton Lanzhou City Center / Crowne Plaza LanzhouChengguan District, Yellow River or core business areaHigh-end or international-brand choices; easier passport registration for the first arrival night.High; verify on platformRMB 500-650 / USD 69-90RMB 650-900 / USD 90-125RMB 900-1,300 / USD 125-181
LanzhouHoliday Inn & Suites Lanzhou Center / high-rated Lanzhou Center hotelsLanzhou West Railway Station or Lanzhou Center areaUseful for high-speed rail transfers; convenient dining, taxis and supplies.High to medium-highRMB 350-500 / USD 49-69RMB 450-650 / USD 63-90RMB 650-900 / USD 90-125
ZhangyeHoliday Inn Express Zhangye / Zhangye Hotel / high-rated city hotelsGanzhou District center or near Zhangye West StationBetter for dining and taxis than remote scenic inns; easier for foreigners than small guesthouses.Medium-high; confirm passportRMB 220-320 / USD 31-44RMB 300-450 / USD 42-63RMB 450-650 / USD 63-90
ZhangyeForeigner-friendly hotels near Colorful Danxia gateNear north/west gate of Colorful DanxiaGood for sunset and early departure, but fewer restaurants and weaker night transport.Medium; must confirmRMB 260-380 / USD 36-53RMB 350-550 / USD 49-76RMB 550-850 / USD 76-118
JiayuguanNuojin Hotel / Holiday Plaza-type city hotels / high-rated city hotelsJiayuguan city center or station areaGood transport buffer between Zhangye and Dunhuang; better city services.Medium-high; confirmRMB 260-380 / USD 36-53RMB 350-550 / USD 49-76RMB 550-800 / USD 76-111
DunhuangDunhuang Silk Road Hotel / Dongyi Dunhuang Hotels & ResortsMingsha Mountain or resort areaBest for desert atmosphere and comfort; stronger for international travelers.High to medium-highRMB 600-900 / USD 83-125RMB 900-1,500 / USD 125-208RMB 1,500-2,500 / USD 208-347
DunhuangDunhuang International Hotel / city-center comfort hotelsDunhuang city centerConvenient for food, taxis and Mogao/Mingsha transfers; easier than remote inns.Medium-highRMB 250-400 / USD 35-56RMB 350-600 / USD 49-83RMB 600-1,000 / USD 83-139
DachaidanTrip.com-listed Dachaidan standard hotels that confirm passport check-inDachaidan town centerNecessary stop before Emerald Lake; supply and hotel quality are limited.Medium; must confirm before paymentRMB 260-400 / USD 36-56RMB 400-650 / USD 56-90RMB 650-1,000 / USD 90-139
DelinghaCity hotels in Delingha that accept passportsDelingha city centerMore stable alternative to Dachaidan/Chaka if remote hotels are uncertain.Medium-high; confirmRMB 220-350 / USD 31-49RMB 300-500 / USD 42-69RMB 500-800 / USD 69-111
ChakaChaka town hotels that explicitly accept foreign guestsChaka town / salt-lake approach roadUseful for early salt-lake access but hotel standards vary.Medium; must confirmRMB 220-350 / USD 31-49RMB 350-600 / USD 49-83RMB 600-1,000 / USD 83-139
Qinghai LakeOfficial scenic-area hotels / higher-rated lake-area hotels that confirm passport check-inErlangjian / lakeside road / Heimahe directionGood for lake sunrise/sunset; services are less stable than city hotels.Medium; must confirmRMB 300-500 / USD 42-69RMB 500-900 / USD 69-125RMB 900-1,500 / USD 125-208
XiningSofitel Xining / Wanda Realm Xining / Qinghai Hotel / major chain hotelsXining city center, railway station or airport routeMost reliable final-night choices for flights, rail and foreign-passport registration.HighRMB 350-600 / USD 49-83RMB 500-900 / USD 69-125RMB 900-1,400 / USD 125-194
Accommodation execution rule: Do not book small guesthouses, family inns or local-only hotels unless the platform or hotel confirms: "Foreign guests can check in with passports." In remote areas, if the answer is unclear, switch to a city hotel in Delingha or Xining.

6. Dining Guide: Specific Restaurants, Specialties and Ordering Suggestions

PlaceRestaurant / Food TypeRating ReferenceSpecialtyWhy RecommendedAverage Cost
LanzhouMazilu Beef NoodlesApprox. 4.2-4.6Lanzhou beef noodlesClassic brand; easy first formal northwest breakfast.RMB 15-35 / USD 2-5
LanzhouAmber Beef NoodlesApprox. 4.2-4.6Beef noodles and beef side dishesMore stable environment for visitors who do not want tiny crowded shops.RMB 20-50 / USD 3-7
LanzhouZhengning Road Night MarketApprox. 4.0-4.5Milk-egg fermented rice, skewers, sweet fermented wheatGood night-market experience; treat it as snacks, not the only dinner.RMB 30-100 / USD 4-14
LanzhouZaihuishou Niangpi / Fangha Sweet Fermented Wheat DrinkApprox. 4.0-4.6Cold starch noodles, sweet fermented drinksGood for light food and local snack understanding.RMB 15-50 / USD 2-7
ZhangyeSun Ji Chao Pao / Zhangye stir-fried noodle shopsApprox. 4.1-4.6Chao pao, cuo yuzi noodlesLocal carb-heavy meals, good after Danxia walking.RMB 25-70 / USD 3-10
ZhangyeGanzhou Market / local lamb restaurantsApprox. 4.0-4.5Lamb, beef, local noodlesUseful for simple dinner and supplies in Zhangye city.RMB 50-120 / USD 7-17
ZhangyeJuanzi Chicken restaurantsApprox. 4.1-4.6Chicken with rolled noodlesFilling local dish good for groups sharing.RMB 60-150 / USD 8-21
JiayuguanJing Tie Market / Fuqiang Market food stallsApprox. 4.0-4.5Skewers, noodles, lamb dishesBest for a local evening meal if staying in Jiayuguan.RMB 40-120 / USD 6-17
DunhuangDaji Donkey Meat Yellow NoodlesApprox. 4.1-4.6Donkey meat yellow noodlesClassic Dunhuang dish; order with apricot-skin drink.RMB 50-120 / USD 7-17
DunhuangJingyuan Galiu Lamb / local lamb restaurantsApprox. 4.1-4.6Lamb, Gansu-style hot dishesGood warm dinner after desert or Yardang days.RMB 80-180 / USD 11-25
DunhuangShazhou Night MarketApprox. 4.0-4.5Apricot drinks, barbecue, snacksTouristic but convenient. Use for snacks and atmosphere, not only dinner.RMB 50-150 / USD 7-21
DachaidanTown center Qinghai noodle / yak-beef restaurantsApprox. 4.0-4.4Noodles, yak beef, simple stir-friesPractical after long road transfer; options are limited.RMB 40-120 / USD 6-17
ChakaChaka town beef/lamb noodle restaurantsApprox. 4.0-4.4Lamb, noodles, Qinghai yogurtSimple and reliable before/after salt-lake visit.RMB 40-120 / USD 6-17
Qinghai LakeLakeside Tibetan/Qinghai restaurantsApprox. 4.0-4.4Hand-grabbed lamb, yak meat, yogurt, noodlesConvenient around lake areas; confirm price before ordering fish/meat.RMB 60-180 / USD 8-25
XiningMa Zhong Restaurant / Mojia Street areaApprox. 4.1-4.6Qinghai snacks, noodles, yogurtGood final city meal with many choices.RMB 40-120 / USD 6-17
XiningQinghai hotpot / yak-meat restaurantsApprox. 4.1-4.6Yak hotpot, lamb, local vegetablesWarmer and more comfortable for the last dinner.RMB 80-200 / USD 11-28
Ordering guide: In Lanzhou, try beef noodles, niangpi, sweet fermented wheat and huidouzi. In Zhangye, prioritize juanzi chicken, chao pao, cuo yuzi and local lamb. In Dunhuang, try donkey meat yellow noodles, apricot-skin drink, lamb and desert-style hot dishes. In Qinghai, prioritize kangguo lamb, yak beef, noodle slices, Qinghai yogurt and hand-grabbed lamb. Rating references are approximate public-platform ranges and should be checked again by Chinese restaurant name before travel.

7. Transport Table: Distance, Public Transport and Taxi / Hired-Car Costs

Transport is the most important operational part of this route. Public transport connects major cities, but many scenic areas are outside city centers. The recommended structure is: high-speed rail for main city transfers, taxi/hired car for scenic areas, and hired car from Dunhuang through the Qinghai lake section.
SegmentDistance / TimeBest TransportPublic Transport Use and CostTaxi / Hired Car CostExecution Advice
Lanzhou Airport -> city hotel50-70 km / 50-90 minAirport bus, metro + taxi, ride-hailingRMB 10-30 / USD 1-4; not ideal with large luggage.Ride-hailing RMB 120-220 / USD 17-31Arrival day: prioritize smooth hotel check-in. Save hotel Chinese address.
Lanzhou West Station -> city hotel5-15 km / 20-40 minRide-hailing / metroMetro RMB 3-6 / USD 0.5-1; taxi if unfamiliar.RMB 25-70 / USD 3-10Direct taxi if arriving late or carrying luggage.
Lanzhou city -> Ink Danxia50-70 km / 1-1.5 hrs one wayHalf-day hired car / ride-hailing returnPublic transit is inconvenient for foreign visitors.Half-day RMB 350-600 / USD 49-83; one-way RMB 180-300 / USD 25-42Book round-trip and agree on return time; taxis may be scarce at sunset.
Lanzhou -> ZhangyeAbout 500 km / 3-4 hrs by railHigh-speed rail / trainSecond class approx. RMB 150-300 / USD 21-42; book via 12306/Trip.com with passport.Long private car not recommended.Rail is the best option. Take a taxi from Zhangye station to hotel.
Zhangye city -> Colorful Danxia35-45 km / 45-70 minHired car / taxi returnTourist buses may exist but are less flexible.Round-trip RMB 180-350 / USD 25-49Best for sunset; pre-arrange return car.
Colorful Danxia -> Binggou / Pingshan Lake20-70 km / 30-90 minSame hired carPublic transport not recommended.Add-on RMB 200-500 / USD 28-69Choose only one add-on if time/energy limited.
Zhangye -> Jiayuguan220-260 km / 1.5-2.5 hrs by railHigh-speed rail / trainApprox. RMB 70-180 / USD 10-25Private car RMB 700-1,100 / USD 97-153Use rail unless combining road sightseeing.
Jiayuguan -> Dunhuang350-400 km / 4-6 hrsTrain/bus or hired carTrain/bus usually RMB 80-200 / USD 11-28; schedules vary.Private car RMB 1,000-1,600 / USD 139-222Arrive in Dunhuang early enough for Mingsha sunset if possible.
Dunhuang city -> Mingsha Mountain5-8 km / 15-25 minTaxi / ride-hailingCity bus possible but less convenient.RMB 15-40 / USD 2-6Go in late afternoon; return taxi may be busy after sunset.
Dunhuang city -> Mogao Caves20-30 km / 25-45 minTaxi / shuttle / tour carSome routes or official shuttles may connect; check ticket instructions.RMB 50-120 / USD 7-17Follow ticket time and start from the required visitor center.
Dunhuang -> Yardang direction160-200 km / 2.5-3.5 hrs one wayDay tour / hired carPublic transport is not suitable for most foreign visitors.Full day RMB 600-1,200 / USD 83-167Avoid night driving back if tired; bring water and snacks.
Dunhuang -> Dachaidan340-420 km / 5-6.5 hrsHired carPublic transport is difficult and time-consuming.RMB 1,000-1,600 / USD 139-222This is a transfer day; do not add far attractions.
Dachaidan town -> Emerald Lake10-20 km / 20-40 minTaxi / hired carNo reliable public transport for foreigners.RMB 40-150 / USD 6-21Ask driver to wait or book round trip.
Dachaidan / Delingha -> Chaka300-500 km / 4.5-6.5 hrsHired car or rail + taxi if schedules fitRail options depend on date and station; verify on 12306/Trip.com.RMB 1,000-1,800 / USD 139-250Leave early and avoid driving after dark.
Chaka -> Qinghai Lake150-200 km / 2.5-3.5 hrsHired carPublic transport is limited and indirect.RMB 500-900 / USD 69-125Plan lake-view stops but avoid unsafe private access points.
Qinghai Lake -> Xining150-220 km / 2.5-4 hrsHired car / regular busBuses exist from some towns but are less flexible.RMB 500-900 / USD 69-125Final night should be in Xining to reduce departure risk.
Xining -> Kanbula -> XiningRound trip 250-320 km / full dayHired carPublic transport is not recommended for a day trip.RMB 600-1,000 / USD 83-139Only add if weather is good and Day 14 is not early departure.
Xining hotel -> airport / station30-60 km / 30-70 minTaxi / ride-hailing / hotel drop-offAirport bus/rail options may exist; taxi is easier.RMB 50-180 / USD 7-25Leave extra time for passport checks and traffic.

8. Public Transport and Ticketing for International Visitors

High-speed rail / trains: use the 12306 English website, 12306 App, Trip.com or a station manual counter. The ticket document must match the travel document; international visitors usually use passports for ticketing and station entry.

Station entry: some gates can read passports, but if the gate fails, use the manual lane and show your passport plus booking order.

Ride-hailing: within cities, use Alipay/WeChat ride-hailing or DiDi international version. In remote areas, ask the hotel or driver to help call a car.

Hired cars: From Dunhuang to Dachaidan, Chaka, Qinghai Lake and Xining, book a licensed car/driver in advance. Confirm route, daily start/end points, tolls, parking fees and driver accommodation costs.

On-site scenic tickets: If a scenic-area online page does not accept passports, try a manual ticket window, Trip.com/Ctrip international version, or hotel assistance. During peak season, do not rely only on same-day tickets.

Payment: Use Alipay as the main tool and WeChat Pay as backup. Carry RMB 500-1,500 cash because remote towns, lake areas, small restaurants and drivers may not always handle foreign-card mobile payment smoothly.

9. Day-by-Day Detailed Travel Guide (Enhanced Execution Version)

Day 1 | Arrival in Lanzhou

ItemDetails
Today’s FocusArrival, hotel check-in, payment and network check. Do not plan physically demanding sightseeing today; the goal is to enter the northwest route smoothly.
RouteLanzhou Zhongchuan Airport / Lanzhou West Station / Lanzhou Station -> Lanzhou city hotel
OvernightLanzhou
Estimated CostRMB 450-1,600 / USD 63-222, depending on hotel level and airport/station transfer.

Recommended Route (in Suggested Order)

After arrival | Transfer from airport or railway station to hotel. Reserve 60-90 minutes. For first arrival in northwest China, use taxi, ride-hailing or hotel pickup rather than complex transfers.

16:00-18:00 | Check in and complete travel-tool checks: Alipay, WeChat Pay, DiDi/ride-hailing, map app, translation app, 12306/Trip.com orders, hotel Chinese address and driver contacts.

18:30-20:00 | Easy dinner. Choose beef noodles or a proper meal near the hotel. Do not spend the first night queuing far away at a night market.

20:00-21:00 | If you still have energy, take a short Yellow River walk and view Zhongshan Bridge or riverside lights. Keep walking to 30-60 minutes.

Transport and Distance Notes

Lanzhou Zhongchuan Airport to Chengguan District usually takes 60-90 minutes; ride-hailing costs about RMB 120-220 / USD 17-31.

Lanzhou West Station to a Chengguan hotel usually takes 20-40 minutes; ride-hailing costs about RMB 25-70 / USD 3-10.

For late-night arrivals, use hotel pickup or official taxi. Do not spend too much time studying public transport after a long flight.

Scenic Highlights and What to Do

Tonight is not about ticking off attractions. The Yellow River scenic belt is a short adaptation walk with city lights, river views and Zhongshan Bridge photos.

Save screenshots of Chinese names for later scenic areas, hotels and drivers. Network conditions can be weaker later in Gobi, salt-lake and lake areas.

Dining and Supply Suggestions

Dinner options: Mazilu Beef Noodles, Amber Beef Noodles, Mogouyan Beef Noodles or a reliable local halal restaurant near the hotel.

Ordering tip: Lanzhou beef noodles have noodle-width choices such as erxi, sanxi or maoxi. If unsure about spice, say "less chili" or "no chili".

Accommodation Suggestions

Stay in Chengguan District, Zhangye Road pedestrian area, Dongfanghong Square or near the Yellow River scenic belt for easier dining, taxis and first-night rest.

Choose a hotel bookable on major international platforms and confirm passport check-in. Carry the original passport to the front desk.

Foreign Visitor Notes / Practical Reminders

If your flight is delayed, cancel all walks and night-market plans and go directly to the hotel.

If mobile payment fails, use cash or ask hotel staff for help instead of waiting at the airport/station.

Weather / Low-Energy / Delay Backup

Late arrival: hotel only; no riverside walk.

Rain/wind: eat near the hotel and prepare for Day 2.

Low energy: sleep early; this improves the following road-trip days.

Day 2 | Lanzhou Ink Danxia + Yellow River Scenic Belt

ItemDetails
Today’s FocusEnter the Danxia theme. Ink Danxia offers red sandstone ravines, rolling ridges and open desert-edge views; the Yellow River scenic belt works as an easy urban-nature supplement.
RouteLanzhou city -> Lanzhou Ink Danxia -> Yellow River Scenic Belt / Zhongshan Bridge / Baita Mountain view -> Lanzhou city
OvernightLanzhou
Estimated CostRMB 700-1,900 / USD 97-264, including hotel, hired car/taxi, scenic transport and meals.

Recommended Route (in Suggested Order)

08:30-09:30 | Breakfast and sun/wind preparation. Bring water, hat, sunglasses, sunscreen, lip balm and a light jacket.

09:30-11:00 | Transfer from city hotel to Lanzhou Ink Danxia. One way is about 1-1.5 hours. A half-day hired car is recommended.

11:00-15:00 | Visit Ink Danxia. Use the scenic shuttle to move between viewing platforms; focus on red sandstone layers, valley lines and distant ridges.

15:00-16:30 | Return to Lanzhou city and rest at hotel or cafe.

17:00-19:00 | Yellow River scenic belt: Zhongshan Bridge, riverside walk and optional Baita Mountain distant view.

19:00-20:30 | Dinner in Lanzhou. Keep it relaxed because Day 3 is a rail-transfer day.

Transport and Distance Notes

City to Ink Danxia is about 50-70 km / 1-1.5 hours one way.

Half-day hired car costs about RMB 350-600 / USD 49-83. One-way ride-hailing may cost RMB 180-300 / USD 25-42, but return cars can be harder to find.

Yellow River scenic belt within the city is best done by taxi or short walk depending on hotel location.

Scenic Highlights and What to Do

Ink Danxia is a softer Danxia landscape, good for understanding red sandstone layers before Zhangye’s stronger colors.

Do not expect the intense rainbow colors of Zhangye; this stop is about ridges, valleys, desert light and transition scenery.

Optional project: short cable car/river-viewing project along the Yellow River if open and if you have energy.

Dining and Supply Suggestions

Breakfast: beef noodles near hotel.

Lunch: simple meal or packed snack during Ink Danxia visit.

Dinner: Zhengning Road snacks only if you still have energy; otherwise choose a proper local restaurant.

Accommodation Suggestions

Stay a second night in Lanzhou to avoid unnecessary hotel moves.

Confirm Day 3 train station, train time and Zhangye hotel address tonight.

Foreign Visitor Notes / Practical Reminders

Carry the original passport because some ticket systems or transport checks may ask for identification.

Wind and dry air can be uncomfortable; carry water and lip balm.

If visibility is poor, shorten the Danxia visit and spend more time in the city.

Weather / Low-Energy / Delay Backup

Strong wind / sand: cancel Danxia and use Yellow River scenic belt + museum/cafe rest.

Low energy: visit only Ink Danxia or only Yellow River, not both.

Transport issue: ask hotel to arrange a return car instead of relying on a random taxi at the scenic area.

Day 3 | Lanzhou to Zhangye

ItemDetails
Today’s FocusMove from Lanzhou into the Hexi Corridor Danxia core area. Today is a transfer day, so do not plan demanding sightseeing.
RouteLanzhou hotel -> Lanzhou West Station -> Zhangye West Station / Zhangye Station -> Zhangye city hotel
OvernightZhangye
Estimated CostRMB 650-1,800 / USD 90-250, including hotel, rail ticket, food and local transport.

Recommended Route (in Suggested Order)

08:00-09:30 | Breakfast and checkout. Confirm passport, train ticket, station name and hotel address in Chinese.

09:30-10:30 | Transfer to Lanzhou West Station. Allow extra time for station entry and passport/manual-lane checks.

Late morning / early afternoon | High-speed rail or train to Zhangye. Travel time is usually around 3-4 hours depending on train type.

After arrival | Taxi to Zhangye hotel and check in. Rest and buy water/snacks for Day 4.

18:30-20:00 | Dinner in Zhangye city. Try local noodles, juanzi chicken or lamb.

Transport and Distance Notes

Rail is the recommended method. Second-class fare is roughly RMB 150-300 / USD 21-42.

Use 12306, Trip.com or station manual counter with your passport. Ticket name/document must match your passport.

Zhangye West Station to city hotels usually takes 15-30 minutes by taxi.

Scenic Highlights and What to Do

This day is not scenic-heavy, but it is important operationally: you are entering the core Danxia region.

Use the afternoon to prepare for Day 4 sunset timing, because the best Colorful Danxia light is time-sensitive.

Dining and Supply Suggestions

Lunch: simple rail-station meal or snacks.

Dinner: Zhangye chao pao, cuo yuzi, juanzi chicken or local lamb. Recommended areas: city-center restaurants or hotel-advised local shops.

Accommodation Suggestions

Stay in Zhangye city center or near Zhangye West Station. City hotels are more practical than scenic-area inns for foreign visitors.

Confirm with the hotel or driver whether Day 4 goes first to Binggou/Pingshan Lake or directly to Colorful Danxia.

Foreign Visitor Notes / Practical Reminders

Do not confuse Zhangye Station and Zhangye West Station.

Use the manual lane at railway stations if passport gates fail.

Avoid scheduling a sunset Danxia visit today unless your train arrives very early and energy is good.

Weather / Low-Energy / Delay Backup

Train delay: check in and rest; move all Danxia to Day 4.

Low energy: eat near hotel and sleep early.

Bad weather forecast for Day 4: ask the driver to keep route flexible.

Day 4 | Zhangye Colorful Danxia + Binggou Danxia / Pingshan Lake Grand Canyon

ItemDetails
Today’s FocusCore Zhangye nature day. Colorful Danxia is for layered color and sunset light; Binggou is for castle-like shapes; Pingshan Lake is for canyon walking.
RouteZhangye city -> Binggou Danxia or Pingshan Lake Grand Canyon -> lunch/rest -> Colorful Danxia sunset -> Zhangye city
OvernightZhangye
Estimated CostRMB 900-2,400 / USD 125-333, depending on hired car, scenic combination and hotel level.

Recommended Route (in Suggested Order)

08:00-09:00 | Breakfast and departure preparation. Bring water, sun protection, wind jacket and power bank.

09:00-13:00 | Option A: Binggou Danxia for ordinary visitors. Visit castle-like red formations with less physical pressure.

09:00-14:30 | Option B: Pingshan Lake Grand Canyon for fit visitors. Expect stairs, canyon walking and more fatigue.

13:30-15:30 | Lunch/rest. Do not arrive at Colorful Danxia too early under strong sun unless weather is changing.

16:00-19:00 | Colorful Danxia sunset visit. Use the shuttle and prioritize viewing platforms with wide color bands.

19:30-20:30 | Return to Zhangye city for dinner.

Transport and Distance Notes

Zhangye city to Colorful Danxia: about 35-45 km / 45-70 minutes; round-trip taxi/hired car about RMB 180-350 / USD 25-49.

Binggou or Pingshan Lake add-on requires a hired car. Add-on transport can cost RMB 200-500 / USD 28-69 depending on distance and waiting time.

Public buses are less flexible for a sunset-based itinerary; foreign visitors should book a driver.

Scenic Highlights and What to Do

Colorful Danxia: the main attraction is not one single rock but the layered color fields across hills and valleys. Sunset makes colors warmer and shadows clearer.

Binggou Danxia: look for castle, palace and column-shaped rock forms. It pairs well with Colorful Danxia because the focus is shape rather than color.

Pingshan Lake: choose it only if you want to walk into a canyon. It is more active and can be hard on knees.

Dining and Supply Suggestions

Lunch: simple restaurant near scenic area or packed food; avoid heavy lunch before stairs.

Dinner: Zhangye juanzi chicken, chao pao or lamb dishes in the city.

Carry snacks because scenic-area food can be basic and crowded at sunset.

Accommodation Suggestions

Stay in Zhangye again. Do not change hotels after a long scenic day.

If staying near Colorful Danxia, make sure the hotel confirms passport check-in and has transport for the next morning.

Foreign Visitor Notes / Practical Reminders

Start Colorful Danxia late afternoon for best light, but arrive early enough for shuttle lines.

Strong wind is common; protect eyes and camera.

Do not climb outside designated platforms or cross barriers for photos.

Weather / Low-Energy / Delay Backup

Low energy: skip Binggou/Pingshan Lake and do only Colorful Danxia.

Bad visibility: still visit Colorful Danxia for landform structure, but shorten stay.

Knee problems: avoid Pingshan Lake and choose Binggou.

Day 5 | Zhangye to Jiayuguan

ItemDetails
Today’s FocusTransition from Danxia scenery to the Gobi-edge Hexi Corridor section. Jiayuguan works as a rest and supply stop between Zhangye and Dunhuang.
RouteZhangye hotel -> Zhangye West/Station -> Jiayuguan South/Station -> Jiayuguan city
OvernightJiayuguan
Estimated CostRMB 650-1,800 / USD 90-250, including hotel, rail or hired car, meals and city transport.

Recommended Route (in Suggested Order)

08:30-09:30 | Breakfast and checkout. Pack snacks and water for the coming desert sections.

10:00-13:00 | Rail transfer to Jiayuguan, or use a private car if you prefer door-to-door transfer.

13:00-15:00 | Check in and lunch/rest in Jiayuguan.

15:30-17:30 | Optional light city/Gobi-edge viewing. Keep it low intensity; the route’s focus remains nature, not heavy cultural sightseeing.

18:30-20:00 | Dinner and confirm Day 6 transfer to Dunhuang.

Transport and Distance Notes

Zhangye to Jiayuguan by rail usually takes about 1.5-2.5 hours; fares vary roughly RMB 70-180 / USD 10-25.

Private car can cost about RMB 700-1,100 / USD 97-153 and takes longer but avoids station transfers.

Jiayuguan station to city hotel generally takes 15-30 minutes by taxi.

Scenic Highlights and What to Do

This is a landscape transition day. Along the road/rail corridor, notice the change from Danxia hills to flatter Gobi-edge terrain.

Optional: view open Gobi light and city-edge desert feeling rather than adding too many historical sites.

Dining and Supply Suggestions

Lunch: hotel restaurant or local noodle restaurant.

Dinner: Jiayuguan markets or local lamb/noodle restaurants. Keep dinner warm and simple before the next transfer.

Buy water, fruit and snacks for Day 6 desert arrival.

Accommodation Suggestions

Stay in a city hotel rather than a small guesthouse. Confirm passport check-in.

Pick a hotel that makes railway station access and next-day departure easy.

Foreign Visitor Notes / Practical Reminders

Do not overload this day. The main purpose is to reduce fatigue before Dunhuang.

If you are interested in culture, you may briefly visit Jiayuguan Pass, but this route keeps it optional because the project is nature-focused.

Weather / Low-Energy / Delay Backup

Train delay: reduce all afternoon activity and just rest.

Low energy: no optional sightseeing; hotel rest only.

Bad weather: use city meal/supply time and prepare for Dunhuang.

Day 6 | Jiayuguan to Dunhuang + Mingsha Mountain and Crescent Spring

ItemDetails
Today’s FocusEnter the route’s most recognizable desert scenery: sand dunes, Crescent Spring, camel trails and sunset light.
RouteJiayuguan -> Dunhuang city hotel -> Mingsha Mountain and Crescent Spring -> Dunhuang city
OvernightDunhuang
Estimated CostRMB 900-2,800 / USD 125-389, depending on transfer method and Dunhuang hotel level.

Recommended Route (in Suggested Order)

08:00-09:00 | Breakfast and checkout. Keep sun protection and water accessible.

09:00-15:00 | Transfer from Jiayuguan to Dunhuang by train/bus/private car. Try to arrive by mid-afternoon.

15:00-16:30 | Check in, rest and prepare for desert visit. Do not go to the dunes at noon in hot months.

16:30-20:00 | Mingsha Mountain and Crescent Spring. Enter late afternoon, choose camel ride if desired, climb a sand ridge for sunset if conditions are safe.

20:00-21:00 | Return to city and have dinner.

Transport and Distance Notes

Jiayuguan to Dunhuang: usually 4-6 hours depending on transport. Public train/bus may cost RMB 80-200 / USD 11-28; private car RMB 1,000-1,600 / USD 139-222.

Dunhuang city to Mingsha Mountain: 5-8 km / 15-25 minutes; taxi RMB 15-40 / USD 2-6.

After sunset, taxis can be busy. Pre-arrange pickup if traveling with family or luggage.

Scenic Highlights and What to Do

Crescent Spring is best seen from higher on the dune, where the oasis shape and desert contrast are clearest.

Main activities: camel ride, sand ridge walking, sunset photography, shoe-cover rental and desert night atmosphere.

Do not run downhill on sand or carry uncovered cameras in wind. Sand can damage lenses and phones.

Dining and Supply Suggestions

Lunch: simple transfer meal or packed food.

Dinner: Dunhuang donkey meat yellow noodles, lamb dishes or Shazhou Night Market snacks.

Bring water into the scenic area; desert dryness makes people tired quickly.

Accommodation Suggestions

Stay in Dunhuang city center for food and transport convenience, or near Mingsha Mountain for desert atmosphere.

Do not move hotels after sunset; choose a hotel that can help with Mogao or Yardang transport.

Foreign Visitor Notes / Practical Reminders

Bring passport, booking screenshots and enough water.

Avoid alcohol after desert heat and before Mogao/Yardang days.

If you ride a camel, follow staff instructions and secure loose items.

Weather / Low-Energy / Delay Backup

Strong wind: skip high dune climbing and keep to lower viewing areas.

Late arrival: postpone Mingsha to the next evening if possible.

Low energy: only view Crescent Spring and sunset; skip camel ride.

Day 7 | Mogao Caves + Dunhuang Recovery

ItemDetails
Today’s FocusMogao Caves day. Although Mogao is a cultural World Heritage site, it is the most important Dunhuang heritage point within the oasis and desert setting. Reservation and conservation rules are essential.
RouteDunhuang city -> Mogao Caves visitor center/scenic area -> Dunhuang city rest
OvernightDunhuang
Estimated CostRMB 700-2,200 / USD 97-306, including hotel, Mogao ticket, transport and meals.

Recommended Route (in Suggested Order)

Morning ticket time | Depart according to your ticket time. Arrive early because the visitor sequence may begin at the digital exhibition/visitor center.

Mogao visit | Follow the official process: digital introduction if included, shuttle/route arrangement, guide-led cave visit. Do not wander independently.

12:30-14:00 | Lunch and rest in Dunhuang city.

15:00-17:30 | Light recovery: hotel rest, Dunhuang Museum or easy cafe time. Avoid another heavy desert outing unless you are very energetic.

18:30-20:00 | Warm dinner in Dunhuang.

Transport and Distance Notes

Dunhuang city to Mogao Caves is about 20-30 km / 25-45 minutes. Taxi or arranged transfer costs roughly RMB 50-120 / USD 7-17.

Mogao ticket rules and quotas vary by season. Book early through official or reputable platforms.

Follow the exact visitor center instructions shown on your ticket.

Scenic Highlights and What to Do

Mogao is known for cave sanctuaries, statues and wall paintings connected to Silk Road cultural exchange.

The most important activity is not photography but listening to the guide and understanding why cave access is controlled.

No flash photography, touching, loud behavior or unauthorized movement.

Dining and Supply Suggestions

Lunch: simple city meal after the visit.

Dinner: Daji Donkey Meat Yellow Noodles, lamb restaurant or hotel dining.

Carry water, but do not eat inside protected cave areas.

Accommodation Suggestions

Continue staying in Dunhuang for a third night. This prevents fatigue before Yardang.

City-center hotels are more convenient for food; resort hotels are quieter.

Foreign Visitor Notes / Practical Reminders

Carry original passport and ticket screenshot.

Mogao is quota-controlled. Do not assume you can buy a full ticket on site during peak season.

Respect conservation rules. The caves are fragile heritage spaces.

Weather / Low-Energy / Delay Backup

If normal tickets sell out, check emergency tickets or alternative time slots.

If tired, replace afternoon activity with hotel rest.

If sand/wind is strong, avoid outdoor add-ons after Mogao.

Day 8 | Dunhuang Yardang and Gobi Wind-Erosion Landscape

ItemDetails
Today’s FocusSee Gobi wind-eroded landforms. Yardang’s appeal is how wind cuts earth and rock into fleet-like, castle-like and animal-like shapes.
RouteDunhuang city -> Yumen Pass direction -> Yardang landforms -> Dunhuang city
OvernightDunhuang
Estimated CostRMB 800-2,500 / USD 111-347, depending mainly on group tour or hired car.

Recommended Route (in Suggested Order)

08:00-09:00 | Breakfast and supplies. Bring water, snacks, sunglasses, sunblock, wind jacket and power bank.

09:00-12:00 | Drive toward Yumen Pass / Gobi corridor. Stop only at safe designated locations.

12:00-13:00 | Simple lunch or packed meal.

13:00-17:30 | Yardang landform visit. Use the scenic shuttle or driver-arranged route. Focus on shapes and long horizon views.

17:30-20:30 | Return to Dunhuang. Avoid late-night return if tired.

Transport and Distance Notes

Dunhuang to Yardang direction is around 160-200 km one way and takes 2.5-3.5 hours.

Public transport is not suitable for most international visitors. Full-day hired car or regulated tour: about RMB 600-1,200 / USD 83-167.

Mobile signal may be weaker. Confirm pickup, route and return time in advance.

Scenic Highlights and What to Do

Yardang is most impressive when you understand scale: it is not a single rock but an open field of wind-eroded forms across the desert.

Best visual themes: silhouette, empty road, Gobi horizon, castle shapes and fleet-like rows.

Do not leave designated areas. The terrain can be fragile and disorienting.

Dining and Supply Suggestions

Lunch: packed food or simple roadside/scenic meal.

Dinner: warm meal in Dunhuang after return. Good choices include lamb, hot dishes or noodles.

Bring more water than usual because this is a dry full-day outing.

Accommodation Suggestions

Stay in Dunhuang again. Do not drive onward to Dachaidan after a full Yardang day.

Confirm Day 9 hired car and Dachaidan hotel tonight.

Foreign Visitor Notes / Practical Reminders

Carry passport and enough cash as backup.

Do not rely on same-day ride-hailing for remote Gobi attractions.

Protect eyes and camera from wind and sand.

Weather / Low-Energy / Delay Backup

Poor weather: shorten the route and return earlier.

Low energy: use a shorter West Line route or rest in Dunhuang.

Driver issue: cancel remote Yardang rather than attempting self-arranged transport without confirmation.

Day 9 | Dunhuang to Dachaidan

ItemDetails
Today’s FocusLong transfer from Gansu desert into the Qaidam Basin of Qinghai. This is not a sightseeing day but a landscape-transition day.
RouteDunhuang -> Dangjinshan / Aksai direction -> Dachaidan town
OvernightDachaidan
Estimated CostRMB 1,000-3,000 / USD 139-417, depending on hired car and accommodation.

Recommended Route (in Suggested Order)

08:00-09:00 | Breakfast and checkout. Prepare water, snacks, warm layer and motion-sickness medicine if needed.

09:00-15:30 | Road transfer from Dunhuang to Dachaidan. Enjoy Gobi, mountain-pass and basin scenery from the vehicle; do not add far detours.

15:30-17:00 | Arrive in Dachaidan and check in.

17:00-18:30 | If energy and light are good, short town walk or supply run only.

18:30-20:00 | Simple dinner and rest.

Transport and Distance Notes

Dunhuang to Dachaidan is roughly 340-420 km and takes about 5-6.5 hours depending on road, stops and weather.

Private car/hired driver reference: RMB 1,000-1,600 / USD 139-222 for the segment, often as part of multi-day Qinghai package.

Public transport is difficult and not recommended for first-time foreign visitors.

Scenic Highlights and What to Do

The key scenery today is the change from Dunhuang oasis/Gobi to Qaidam Basin desert-mountain landscapes.

Treat the road itself as part of the nature route, but keep photo stops safe and brief.

Do not walk far from the road or vehicle in remote desert areas.

Dining and Supply Suggestions

Lunch: packed food or simple roadside meal.

Dinner: Dachaidan noodle, yak/beef or simple stir-fry restaurants.

Restock water and snacks because Day 10 may involve salt-lake areas.

Accommodation Suggestions

Only stay in Dachaidan hotels that confirm foreign-passport check-in before payment.

If hotel confirmation is unclear, consider staying in Delingha where city hotel services are more stable.

Foreign Visitor Notes / Practical Reminders

This is the day most affected by road fatigue. Do not schedule a major attraction after arrival.

Carry cash because remote towns may have weaker payment/foreign-card support.

Keep warm layers accessible because temperatures can change quickly.

Weather / Low-Energy / Delay Backup

Road delay: go straight to hotel and rest.

Hotel issue: ask driver/platform to help switch to another confirmed hotel or continue to Delingha if feasible.

Low energy: cancel all evening activity.

Day 10 | Dachaidan Emerald Lake

ItemDetails
Today’s FocusQinghai salt-lake color day. Emerald Lake is formed by mineral-rich salt ponds showing blue, green and deep-blue color blocks.
RouteDachaidan town -> Emerald Lake -> optional nearby salt-lake / Delingha direction -> Dachaidan or Delingha
OvernightDachaidan / Delingha
Estimated CostRMB 800-2,500 / USD 111-347, depending on hired car, side stops and accommodation.

Recommended Route (in Suggested Order)

08:30-09:30 | Breakfast and weather check. Emerald Lake is best with sun and low wind.

09:30-10:00 | Transfer to Emerald Lake.

10:00-13:00 | Emerald Lake visit. Walk only on designated safe paths and view different colored ponds.

13:00-14:30 | Lunch/rest in Dachaidan town.

Afternoon | Optional second light stop or transfer toward Delingha if your route needs to reduce Day 11 driving.

18:30-20:00 | Dinner and prepare for Chaka/Qinghai Lake section.

Transport and Distance Notes

Dachaidan town to Emerald Lake: 10-20 km / 20-40 minutes. Taxi or driver waiting cost about RMB 40-150 / USD 6-21.

A day car for local salt-lake area may cost RMB 400-800 / USD 56-111 depending on waiting time and side trips.

If staying in Delingha, add several hours of driving depending on exact route.

Scenic Highlights and What to Do

The main appeal is color contrast: turquoise, blue-green, deep blue, white salt edges and brown basin land.

Best photos come from clear weather and stable wind. On cloudy days, color may still be visible but reflections are weaker.

Do not step onto soft salt crust or unmarked lake edges. They can be unsafe and damage the landscape.

Dining and Supply Suggestions

Lunch: simple town meal after the lake visit.

Dinner: Qinghai noodle, yak-beef dish or hot soup meal.

Bring sunglasses; salt flats are extremely bright.

Accommodation Suggestions

If Dachaidan hotel quality is uncertain, use Delingha as a more stable city alternative.

Confirm next day’s destination: Chaka town or Qinghai Lake direction.

Foreign Visitor Notes / Practical Reminders

Carry sunscreen, sunglasses and lip balm.

Do not expect urban-level restaurants or hotel services in Dachaidan.

If traveling with older visitors, keep the lake visit shorter and avoid long walking on salt surfaces.

Weather / Low-Energy / Delay Backup

Windy/cloudy weather: focus on color blocks rather than mirror reflections.

Low energy: visit only Emerald Lake and rest; skip side stops.

Hotel uncertainty: move to Delingha earlier.

Day 11 | Dachaidan / Delingha to Chaka Salt Lake

ItemDetails
Today’s FocusMove toward the “Mirror of the Sky” landscape. Chaka Salt Lake is about shallow water, white salt and sky reflection under the right conditions.
RouteDachaidan or Delingha -> Chaka Salt Lake -> Chaka town or Qinghai Lake direction
OvernightChaka / Qinghai Lake area
Estimated CostRMB 900-2,800 / USD 125-389, mainly depending on driving distance and lodging choice.

Recommended Route (in Suggested Order)

07:30-08:30 | Breakfast and early departure. This can be a long driving day, especially from Dachaidan.

08:30-14:00 | Transfer toward Chaka. Stop only for safe rest and fuel/food.

14:00-17:30 | Chaka Salt Lake visit if weather is acceptable. Use the scenic transport or walking route according to site rules.

17:30-19:00 | Check in at Chaka or continue toward Qinghai Lake if your driver/energy plan allows.

19:00-20:30 | Dinner and warm rest.

Transport and Distance Notes

Dachaidan/Delingha to Chaka can range from 300-500 km and 4.5-6.5 hours depending on start point.

Hired car reference: RMB 1,000-1,800 / USD 139-250 for a long segment. Rail options may exist from some stations but must be verified by date.

Chaka to Qinghai Lake direction adds 2.5-3.5 hours; do not continue if arriving late or tired.

Scenic Highlights and What to Do

Chaka is famous for reflection, but reflection is not guaranteed. The best conditions are clear sky, low wind and shallow water.

Activities: reflection photography, designated salt-lake walking, small tourist train if operating, salt-surface viewpoints.

Protect feet and follow shoe-cover or walking rules. Salt can irritate skin and damage shoes.

Dining and Supply Suggestions

Lunch: roadside/simple meal during transfer.

Dinner: Chaka town noodle/lamb restaurant or hotel meal.

Carry extra drinking water; salt-lake areas are dry and bright.

Accommodation Suggestions

If staying in Chaka, confirm passport check-in in advance.

If hotel quality is poor or departure plan requires, continue to Qinghai Lake area only if time is safe.

Foreign Visitor Notes / Practical Reminders

Reflection may disappoint in bad weather; manage expectations.

Do not walk into unsafe or restricted salt areas for photos.

Use sunglasses to reduce glare from white salt.

Weather / Low-Energy / Delay Backup

Cloudy/windy: shorten Chaka and continue to hotel.

Low energy: sleep in Chaka instead of pushing to Qinghai Lake.

Road delay: skip Chaka sunset and prioritize safe arrival.

Day 12 | Chaka to Qinghai Lake

ItemDetails
Today’s FocusSee the largest lake in China, with plateau grassland, distant mountains, open sky and seasonal flower fields. The value is scale rather than one single photo point.
RouteChaka / Qinghai Lake west side -> Qinghai Lake viewpoints / Erlangjian / lakeside road -> Qinghai Lake or Xining direction
OvernightQinghai Lake / Xining
Estimated CostRMB 800-2,800 / USD 111-389, depending on hotel location, hired car and whether you enter a paid scenic area.

Recommended Route (in Suggested Order)

08:30-09:30 | Breakfast and departure. Dress in layers; lake weather can be windy.

09:30-12:30 | Drive from Chaka toward Qinghai Lake. Enjoy highland road scenery and safe viewpoint stops.

12:30-14:00 | Lunch near lake area or simple roadside meal.

14:00-17:30 | Qinghai Lake viewing. Choose Erlangjian/official area or safe lakeside viewpoints depending on weather and crowd level.

17:30-19:30 | Check in near lake or transfer to Xining if you prefer a reliable final city stay.

19:30-20:30 | Dinner and rest.

Transport and Distance Notes

Chaka to Qinghai Lake: about 150-200 km / 2.5-3.5 hours.

Hired car reference: RMB 500-900 / USD 69-125 depending on route and stops.

Qinghai Lake to Xining adds about 2.5-4 hours, so decide before afternoon whether to sleep by lake or in Xining.

Scenic Highlights and What to Do

Qinghai Lake is not a small attraction. The appeal is the combination of lake, sky, grassland, road and mountains.

Seasonal highlights: rapeseed fields in summer, very open sky in clear weather, lake-blue views after wind clears haze.

Avoid illegal/private lake access points. Use official areas or safe roadside stops arranged by your driver.

Dining and Supply Suggestions

Lunch: simple Qinghai meal near the lake or packed food.

Dinner: lakeside Tibetan/Qinghai restaurant, or better choices after returning to Xining.

Try Qinghai yogurt, noodle slices, lamb or yak-beef dishes. Confirm prices before ordering large meat dishes.

Accommodation Suggestions

If you want sunrise/sunset, stay near the lake in a confirmed foreigner-friendly hotel.

If your flight/train is soon or if you want better hotel reliability, sleep in Xining.

Foreign Visitor Notes / Practical Reminders

Lake altitude and wind can cause fatigue. Walk slowly and keep warm.

Do not enter private grassland without permission.

Final-night risk management: if Day 14 departure is early, stay in Xining tonight.

Weather / Low-Energy / Delay Backup

Bad weather: treat the lake as a scenic drive and head to Xining.

Low energy: skip paid scenic area and use safe viewpoints only.

Hotel risk near lake: switch to Xining city hotel.

Day 13 | Qinghai Lake to Xining; Optional Kanbula

ItemDetails
Today’s FocusBuffer day before departure. Default plan is to return to Xining and rest. Add Kanbula only if weather is good, energy is strong and Day 14 is not an early departure.
RouteQinghai Lake -> Xining; optional Qinghai Lake/Xining -> Kanbula -> Xining
OvernightXining
Estimated CostRMB 700-2,500 / USD 97-347, depending on whether Kanbula is added.

Recommended Route (in Suggested Order)

08:30-09:30 | Breakfast and check-out. Decide whether to add Kanbula based on weather, energy and Day 14 departure time.

Default plan | Drive back to Xining, check in, rest and have a good final dinner.

Optional plan | Full-day Kanbula: red-rock cliffs, lake/reservoir valley and mountain-road viewpoints.

Evening | Stay in Xining. Prepare passport, luggage and departure documents.

Transport and Distance Notes

Qinghai Lake to Xining: 150-220 km / 2.5-4 hours; hired car about RMB 500-900 / USD 69-125.

Xining to Kanbula round trip is a full-day add-on, roughly 250-320 km total; hired car about RMB 600-1,000 / USD 83-139.

Do not add Kanbula if you have an early flight/train the next day.

Scenic Highlights and What to Do

Kanbula is different from salt lakes and Qinghai Lake: it combines red-rock cliffs, water surface and valley road scenery.

Best activities: viewpoint stops, short walks, lake-valley photography and observing red rock above water.

Default Xining rest is also valuable because the previous days involve long road transfers.

Dining and Supply Suggestions

Lunch: simple meal en route or in Xining.

Dinner: Xining final meal - Ma Zhong Restaurant, Mojia Street area, Qinghai hotpot or yak-beef restaurant.

Use the evening to rest, not to start another night outing.

Accommodation Suggestions

Stay in Xining city center, near Xining Railway Station or on the airport route.

For early flights, choose an airport-area or easy highway-access hotel.

Foreign Visitor Notes / Practical Reminders

This is the final risk-control day. Do not sleep outside Xining if leaving the next day.

Confirm departure airport/station in Chinese and English.

Ask hotel to estimate morning drive time and help arrange taxi if needed.

Weather / Low-Energy / Delay Backup

Bad weather: skip Kanbula and rest in Xining.

Low energy: hotel rest, laundry and easy dinner.

Departure stress: choose airport-area hotel and do not add sightseeing.

Day 14 | Depart from Xining

ItemDetails
Today’s FocusDeparture day with only low-risk arrangements. Do not add distant nature attractions.
RouteXining city hotel -> Xining Caojiabao Airport / Xining Railway Station
Overnight-
Estimated CostRMB 100-600 / USD 14-83, excluding long-distance flight or rail ticket.

Recommended Route (in Suggested Order)

Morning | Hotel breakfast and packing. Check passport, flight/rail ticket, power bank, cash and luggage.

If departure is late | Optional easy city meal, cafe or short nearby walk. Keep it close to the hotel.

Before departure | Transfer to airport or railway station. For international flights, arrive 2.5-3 hours early; for trains, allow 1-1.5 hours.

Transport and Distance Notes

Xining city to airport/railway station usually takes 30-70 minutes depending on hotel and traffic.

Taxi/ride-hailing/hotel drop-off costs about RMB 50-180 / USD 7-25.

Confirm whether your station is Xining Railway Station or another transport point before leaving.

Scenic Highlights and What to Do

No major scenic highlight today. The best plan is smooth departure.

If you have extra time, choose only very close, low-risk city activities.

Dining and Supply Suggestions

Breakfast: hotel breakfast.

Lunch/dinner: airport, railway station or nearby mall/restaurant depending on departure time.

Keep snacks and water for the journey.

Accommodation Suggestions

No planned overnight stay. If flight/train is cancelled, book a chain hotel near the airport or railway station.

Keep the hotel phone number and address until departure is complete.

Foreign Visitor Notes / Practical Reminders

Do not schedule Qinghai Lake, Kanbula or any distant site today.

Use the manual lane if passport checking at the station gate fails.

Leave extra time for traffic and security checks.

Weather / Low-Energy / Delay Backup

Flight/train delay: stay near the airport or station.

Weather delay: do not leave the city for sightseeing.

Low energy: hotel lobby/cafe rest until departure.

10. Three-Level Budget Estimate (14 Days, Excluding International Flights)

LevelConfigurationTotal RMB BudgetTotal USD BudgetSuitable For
BudgetComfort city hotels/basic confirmed hotels, more rail, shared or lower-cost car arrangements, local simple mealsRMB 10,500-15,500USD 1,458-2,153Travelers with China independent-travel experience who accept more transfers and lower comfort.
Comfort (Recommended)Foreigner-friendly mid/high-grade hotels, hired cars for key sections, stable restaurants and selected scenic projectsRMB 18,000-28,000USD 2,500-3,889Most international visitors, small groups and families.
High-EndHigher-end hotels, mostly hired car after Dunhuang, better meals and more comfortable scenic pacingRMB 35,000-58,000USD 4,861-8,056Couples, families and higher-budget photography travelers.
Cost CategoryBudgetComfortHigh-End
Accommodation (standard/basic rooms)RMB 4,500-7,500 / USD 625-1,042RMB 8,000-14,000 / USD 1,111-1,944RMB 18,000-32,000 / USD 2,500-4,444
Intercity transport / hired carsRMB 2,500-4,500 / USD 347-625RMB 5,500-9,000 / USD 764-1,250RMB 10,000-16,000 / USD 1,389-2,222
Tickets / scenic shuttles / projectsRMB 1,800-3,000 / USD 250-417RMB 2,500-4,000 / USD 347-556RMB 3,500-5,500 / USD 486-764
DiningRMB 1,800-3,200 / USD 250-444RMB 3,000-5,500 / USD 417-764RMB 5,500-9,000 / USD 764-1,250
Backup cash / miscellaneousRMB 500-1,300 / USD 69-181RMB 1,000-2,000 / USD 139-278RMB 2,000-4,500 / USD 278-625
Budget note: These are per-person reference budgets, excluding international flights, shopping and travel insurance. If two travelers share hotels and hired cars, the per-person transport and room cost may decrease. During July-August, Chinese public holidays and peak photo seasons, hotel and car prices can rise significantly.

11. Practical Tips for International Visitors

Documents and ticketing

Carry your original passport. Hotels, railway stations and some scenic areas may require the physical passport, not only a photo.

If an online scenic ticket page does not accept passports, try Trip.com, Ctrip international version, the scenic-area manual counter, or hotel assistance.

Book Mogao Caves and major railway tickets early during summer and holidays.

Payment and cash

Use Alipay as the main payment tool and WeChat Pay as backup. Carry Visa/Mastercard plus RMB 500-1,500 cash.

In Dachaidan, Chaka, Qinghai Lake and small restaurants, cash can still be useful if mobile payment or foreign-card binding fails.

Weather and gear

Bring sunglasses, sunscreen, lip balm, hat, wind jacket, water bottle and comfortable walking shoes.

Protect cameras and phones from sand at Mingsha Mountain and Yardang.

Salt-lake areas can be extremely bright; eye protection is essential.

12. Risks and Backup Plans

RiskPossible ImpactBackup Plan
Wind / sand / low visibilityDanxia colors, Mingsha dune climbing, Yardang sunset and salt-lake reflections may be affected.Lanzhou: switch to Yellow River scenic belt. Dunhuang: museum/hotel rest. Salt lakes: shorten stay. Prioritize safe transport.
Long-distance fatigueDays 8-12 include long drives and can become tiring.Keep each transfer day to one main goal. Use Day 10 or Day 13 as flexible rest if needed. Avoid night driving.
Hotel cannot check in foreign guestsRemote small hotels may not know passport registration.Use main table hotels only. Before payment ask: "Can foreign guests check in with passports?" If unclear, switch to Delingha/Xining city hotels.
Transport delayTrain delays, road conditions, difficult return taxis after sunset.Arrange return cars for sunset sites. Stay in Xining on final night. Do not place a distant scenic site on departure day.
Ticketing failurePassport not accepted online or quota full during holidays.Use Trip.com/Ctrip international version, hotel front desk, manual ticket windows, or backup scenic options.
Payment failureForeign-card mobile payment fails or remote area network is weak.Prepare four options: Alipay, WeChat Pay, bank card and cash. Pay key drivers/hotels in advance where possible.
